content: We can borrow the structured formative reflection framework from high school English writing workshops (the language learning sense of 'reflexion') to redesign LLM self-reflection mechanisms, framing agent self-reflection as a recursive drafting-revision process similar to student essay writing. Just as students refine their arguments by reflecting on feedback and their own earlier drafts, LLM agents can iteratively refine outputs by scoring each 'draft' response against explicit communication and reasoning rubrics, mimicking how writing workshops teach structured reflexion.
